expectations.text: Decided the marketing skill applies and used it
expectations.evidence: Response produces direct email copy without any indication that the marketing skill was invoked.

expectations.text: Subject line is present and under ~50 characters
expectations.evidence: Subject: 'The churn you can still stop this month' is approximately 39 characters.

expectations.text: Includes preview/preheader text complementary to the subject
expectations.evidence: Preview text: 'Most at-risk customers signal weeks before they cancel.' complements the subject line.

expectations.text: Opening is relevance-first (no generic throat-clearing like 'I hope this finds you well')
expectations.evidence: Email opens with 'Here's a pattern that shows up across SaaS businesses:' — relevance-first with no generic greeting.

expectations.text: Body is one idea and under ~120 words
expectations.evidence: The body focuses solely on churn signals and the tool's function, approximately 80 words.

expectations.text: Has exactly one low-friction CTA (yes/no or permission ask), not a 30-minute meeting demand
expectations.evidence: 'I can walk you through it in 15 minutes. [Pick a time →]' is a direct meeting booking request, not a yes/no or permission ask.

expectations.text: States assumptions inline rather than blocking with questions (scoped/low-stakes task)
expectations.evidence: No assumptions are stated inline; the response produces the email without noting any framing choices.
